# Integration Checklist: Prerequisite and Documentation Messaging

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality for Claude/Codex prerequisite guidance parity, capability-first integration boundaries, concrete identifier exceptions, generated payload handling, and repo/vendor evidence separation.
**Created**: 2026-06-19
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Cross-Platform Guidance Contract

- [x] CHK001 Are Claude and Codex prerequisite guidance surfaces both named as integration peers that must express the same capability-first contract? [Resolved, Spec FR-004, Spec Reviewability Notes, Plan Declared File Operations]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the setup-facing capability categories consistent across prerequisite output, Claude guidance, Codex guidance, and adjacent coach/autopilot guidance? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications Session 1, Research Decision 2, Plan Phase 0 Research]
- [ ] CHK003 Is fallback and escalation behavior specified consistently across prerequisite output and active guidance? [Consistency, Spec FR-003, Spec FR-012, Plan PR Review Packet Traceability]

## Concrete Identifier Boundary

- [ ] CHK004 Are concrete optional capability or provider identifiers limited to platform metadata, exact file references, historical provenance, or source-regenerated payloads? [Clarity, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-010]
- [ ] CHK005 Does the specification distinguish active behavior surfaces from metadata, generated, fixture, archive, changelog, and provenance surfaces? [Consistency, Spec Reviewability Notes, Capability Discovery Reference Metadata Policy]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the review packet required to classify any remaining concrete optional-tool names by allowed exception? [Completeness, Spec PR Review Packet Requirements]

## Generated Payload Integration

- [ ] CHK007 Are generated payload copies excluded from hand edits unless a source change requires parity regeneration? [Completeness, Spec FR-010, Research Decision 4, Plan Implementation Approach]
- [ ] CHK008 Does the plan define how source regeneration evidence will be recorded when generated payloads need parity? [Measurability, Plan PR Review Packet Traceability, Quickstart PR Packet Checklist]

## Repo And Platform Evidence Boundary

- [ ] CHK009 Are repository-specific guidance claims required to cite Racecraft sources while platform or vendor behavior claims require official vendor evidence? [Completeness, Spec PR Review Packet Requirements, Spec Clarifications Session 2]
- [x] CHK010 Does the plan map repo-vs-platform guidance separation to verification or review evidence rather than leaving it only as PR prose? [Resolved, Plan Implementation Approach, Plan PR Review Packet Traceability]

## Scope And Deferred Enforcement

- [ ] CHK011 Are integration boundaries clear enough to prevent TACD-003 from reworking TACD-002 behavior or TACD-004 enforcement? [Scope, Spec FR-007, Spec FR-009, Plan Implementation Approach]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: Claude/Codex prerequisite parity, capability-first contract consistency, concrete identifier exceptions, source-regenerated payload handling, and repo/vendor evidence separation.
- Depth level: standard reviewer checklist.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer before task generation or implementation.
